citations.description: show_ticket returned comments: [] — no attestation of any kind is present on the ticket.

findings.criterion: [operator-attested] one-time repair of the already-inverted Jira links (neither diff self-corrects them).
findings.detail: This criterion requires a concrete attestation in the ticket (a repair change/PR/deploy id, observed outcome, or timestamp) confirming the already-inverted Jira links were manually corrected. The ticket has zero comments and no such attestation anywhere in the ticket record.
findings.remediation: Record proof as a ticket comment naming: (a) the specific Jira issues repaired and how (script/manual fix/repair run), (b) a reference id or observed Jira state confirming the links now point correctly (B Blocks A), and (c) when this was performed.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
